deployment_question: How does the model perform as drug discovery moves forward into newer or more novel compounds?
definition: Use k-fold n-step-forward validation or related sequential designs to train on earlier/less novel bioactivity records and evaluate on later/more novel records.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: samples ordered operating points along time or novelty

implementation_recipe: Order records by assay date, publication time, project stage, or novelty rank.
Train on records before the step and evaluate on the next held-out step.
Repeat across folds and report degradation across steps.
quality_gates: Make the order variable explicit and auditable.
Control for assay and target leakage.
Report chemical similarity between each future step and training data.
failure_modes: Dates can be missing or inconsistent across assays.
Later records may differ in assay protocol as well as chemical novelty.
Retrospective curation can leak future knowledge.

citations.title: Step Forward Cross Validation for Bioactivity Prediction
citations.evidence: Tests k-fold n-step-forward cross-validation and reports better OOD validation insight than random splits.

citations.title: Benchmarking compound activity prediction for real-world drug discovery applications
citations.evidence: Constructs a real-world compound activity benchmark with assay-level performance variation.
